title: Create Admin Dashboard Blog Management UI
description: Build React admin dashboard pages for managing blog posts with full CRUD functionality.
details: In the frontend (apps/frontend), add admin dashboard routes and components for listing, creating, editing, and deleting blog posts. Use forms for create/edit, and tables/lists for display. Ensure UI is accessible and follows project styling conventions. Integrate JWT authentication for admin access.
test_strategy: Test UI manually and with React Testing Library. Verify all CRUD flows, form validation, and error handling. Confirm only authenticated admins can access these pages.

title: Integrate TanStack Query for Blog Data Fetching and Mutations
description: Use TanStack Query (React Query) for client-side data fetching and mutations in the admin dashboard.
details: Replace direct fetch calls in the admin dashboard blog components with TanStack Query hooks (useQuery, useMutation). Implement cache invalidation and optimistic updates for a responsive UX. Handle loading and error states according to best practices.
test_strategy: Test all CRUD operations in the UI to ensure data is fetched, cached, and updated correctly. Simulate network errors and verify error handling. Check that UI updates reflect backend changes immediately.

title: Implement Blog API CRUD Endpoints
description: Develop RESTful API endpoints for blog post CRUD operations in the Node.js backend.
details: In the Express.js API (apps/api), create routes under /api/admin/posts for Create, Read (list & single), Update, and Delete operations. Use TypeScript for type safety. Ensure endpoints are protected with JWT authentication and validate input data. Integrate with the D1 database using the schema from Task 1.
test_strategy: Write unit and integration tests for each endpoint using a test database. Test authentication, input validation, and all CRUD operations. Use Postman or similar tools for manual verification.

title: Design and Implement Blog Database Schema & Migrations
description: Define the database schema for blog posts and implement migration scripts for Cloudflare D1.
details: Create a 'posts' table with fields such as id (primary key), title, content, author_id, created_at, updated_at, and status (e.g., draft/published). Write migration scripts compatible with Cloudflare D1 and ensure versioning and rollback support. Place migration scripts in the appropriate migration directory as per project conventions.
test_strategy: Run migration scripts against a test D1 instance. Verify table creation, field types, and rollback functionality. Attempt to insert and query sample data to confirm schema correctness.
